summary.intent: Address three Codex review threads on PR #681 for the TimeField component by adding regression tests, patching the implementation, and validating with the full quality gate pipeline.
summary.outcome: All three Codex review issues were fixed (controlled clear ambiguity, uncontrolled range clamping, CJK H11 day-period fallback), tests pass, clippy/fmt/spec/proptest/coverage checks are green, mutation testing completed with 0 missed mutants, and the review-response commit was pushed.

repo: The project uses a multi-stage quality gate: cargo fmt (nightly), cargo clippy, cargo test (unit + spec_conformance + proptest), cargo llvm-cov, cargo mutants, and cargo xtask spec validate — all must pass before a review-response commit.
Codex reviews are triggered via @codex review comment on the PR and tracked by GitHub reactions: 👀 means actively reviewing, 👍 means approved, absence of 👀 with threads means actionable feedback.
The mutation config lives at .cargo/mutants.toml and supports narrow per-function timeout exclusions to triage slow-running mutants that are already covered but time out the test harness.
Spec files live at spec/components/date-time/time-field.md and must be updated alongside implementation changes; cargo xtask spec validate enforces conformance.

The TimeField sync_props logic handles both controlled and uncontrolled modes, with day-period fallback behavior differing between H11 and H12 calendar systems — a subtle invariant not obvious from the code.

code.finding: sync_props was ambiguous for controlled None: did not distinguish between 'clear the field' and 'no value provided'. Fixed to treat controlled None as an explicit clear.
Uncontrolled range changes (min_value/max_value prop updates) did not clamp the current internal value to the new bounds. Fixed in sync_props.
time_segment_aria_label empty-string mutant consistently times out in cargo-mutants despite being covered by snapshot/attr tests — added to .cargo/mutants.toml timeout exclusion.

CJK day-period timeout fallback (day_period_from_cjk_buffer) needed H11-specific disambiguation to avoid PM flip. The fix preserves current day-period only for H11, not H12, to avoid breaking the existing H12 timeout fallback test.

workflow: Run focused tests (cargo test -p ars-components date_time::time_field --lib) before and after patching to confirm tests fail on the broken implementation and pass after the fix — this validates the tests are meaningful.
cargo-mutants --iterate mode re-uses a prior baseline and skips previously caught/unviable mutants, making iteration faster for incremental review fixes.
Monitoring mutation progress via mutants.out/{caught,missed,timeout,unviable}.txt files provides visibility without waiting for stdout when the run is long.
Match on PR reaction polling (👀 presence/absence) is more reliable than time-based stall detection for Codex review state; stall threshold is ~15 minutes.
Clippy may flag match-arm shape issues (redundant None arms) introduced during targeted fixes — merge arms without changing behavior as a cleanup step before committing.

friction: Codex review took many polling cycles (10+ minutes) before posting threads, requiring a long busy-wait loop with 120s sleep intervals.
cargo-mutants iteration over a large file (time_field/mod.rs) took extremely long — dozens of polling cycles to complete, with no stdout progress between mutants.
One mutant (time_segment_aria_label -> String::new()) consistently timed out despite being covered, requiring a manual triage step and config exclusion rather than a clean 0-timeout result.
The H11 day-period fix initially over-preserved current day-period for H12 as well, breaking an existing H12 test — required a narrowing patch to scope the fix to H11 only.
nightly rustfmt found formatting issues in the review fix after all other checks passed, requiring an extra fmt + recheck cycle before the final commit.
open_items: The time_segment_aria_label timeout exclusion in .cargo/mutants.toml is a workaround — worth investigating whether the test harness can be made faster for that mutant to remove the exclusion.

The controlled None / uncontrolled None ambiguity fix in Props may warrant a broader audit of other date-time components (e.g., date_field) for the same pattern, as the same sync_props structure appears there.

Codex review did not produce a 👍 approval reaction after the fix was pushed — the session ends at commit/push, so it is unclear whether Codex will re-review the follow-up commit automatically or requires a new @codex review trigger.
